screening_results.reason: This is a narrative review on hemodynamic management of septic shock, not an original task-based fMRI study. It involves clinical septic patients (not healthy adults) and reports no fMRI task-related brain activation. Exclusion criteria E1, E3, and E4 apply.

Abstract explicitly describes the paper as a review of fluid reasoning and neural underpinnings across development; it does not report original task-based fMRI experiments or task-related activation results in healthy adults. Therefore it meets exclusion criterion E1 (review/meta-analysis).

Abstract describes a theoretical/review paper on individual differences in working memory (correlational approaches). It is not an original task-based fMRI study and does not report task-related brain activation results, so it meets exclusion criteria.

This publication is a systematic review and meta-analysis on health literacy and aging, not an original task-based fMRI experiment. It therefore meets exclusion criterion E1 (review/meta-analysis) and does not report task-related fMRI results in healthy adults.

This is a narrative review about B cells and multiple sclerosis, not a task-based fMRI study of problem solving in healthy adults. It does not involve fMRI data, problem-solving tasks, or healthy adult participants.

This is an evidence-based guideline/review on surgical management of idiopathic normal-pressure hydrocephalus, not a task-based fMRI study of problem solving in healthy adults. It does not report fMRI task activation or include healthy adult problem-solving tasks.

Abstract describes a functional connectivity density (resting-state/connectivity) study correlating FCD with Raven scores; no task-based fMRI or task-related activation is reported. Meets exclusion criterion E2 (resting-state or connectivity-only).

Abstract describes ethical and nursing discussion about artificial nutrition and hydration; it is not an fMRI study nor does it report task-based neuroimaging of problem-solving in healthy adults. Therefore it meets the exclusion criterion for non-fMRI studies.

This is a behavioral developmental study in children (ages 3.6–6), not a task-based fMRI study of adults. It does not involve fMRI and has no healthy adult sample, so it does not meet the review inclusion criteria.

Event-related task-based fMRI study using a relational reasoning (Raven-like) task, includes a healthy adult group (ages 18–25), and reports task-related brain activation (RLPFC, DLPFC, parietal). Does not meet any exclusion criteria.

Abstract describes behavioral experiments on brief cognitive and social-psychological interventions and problem-solving performance, with no mention of fMRI data collection, task-based imaging, or reported brain activation results. Therefore this is a non-fMRI study.

This is a behavioral cognitive-training trial using a mobile video-game; the abstract contains no mention of fMRI data acquisition or task-related brain activation results. Therefore it is a non-fMRI study and does not meet the review objective.

This is a clinical review about lumbar puncture and CT, not an empirical task-based fMRI study of problem solving in healthy adults. It is a review and non-fMRI, so it does not meet inclusion criteria.

This abstract describes a review/meta-analysis of videogame interventions and cognitive transfer rather than reporting a task-based fMRI experiment. It does not report task-related brain activation in healthy adults. Excluded based on E1 (review/meta-analysis) and E4 (non-fMRI study).

This is a handbook chapter/review on clinical evaluation of work-related neurologic disorders; it is not a task-based fMRI study, does not involve problem-solving tasks or report task-based brain activation in healthy adults, and is a non-fMRI review.

Abstract makes no mention of neuroimaging or fMRI methods and appears to discuss fluid intelligence and psychosocial outcomes conceptually/behaviorally. Therefore it is likely a non-fMRI study and does not meet the fMRI requirement.

This is a meta-analysis of executive dysfunction in obstructive sleep apnea patients, not an original task-based fMRI study of problem-solving in healthy adults. It focuses on patient samples rather than healthy adults and does not report task-related fMRI activation, meeting exclusion criteria E1, E3, and E4.
Participants are children (ages 8–16) with ADHD; the study is not an fMRI task-based study and does not include a healthy adult sample. Therefore it does not meet the review inclusion criteria.

Study samples children (ages 5–12), not adults, and is a behavioral developmental study without any fMRI data. Therefore it does not include a healthy adult fMRI sample or report task-related brain activation.

This is a review/risk–benefit assessment about hyaluronan injections for osteoarthritis, not a task-based fMRI study of problem solving. It does not involve healthy adult participants performing problem-solving tasks and reports no task-related brain activation results.

This is an in vitro study using prostate cancer DU145 cells examining molecular effects of IP6; it does not involve human participants or task-based fMRI, so it fails inclusion. Exclude for non-fMRI and no healthy adult sample.

Study tested 4-year-old children in a behavioral training intervention and did not use fMRI. It therefore lacks a healthy adult sample and is not a task-based fMRI study. Exclusion criteria E3 and E4 apply.

Abstract describes a behavioral cognitive aging study using the Tower of London task and reports no fMRI methods or task-related brain activation. This is a non-fMRI study, so it fails the fMRI requirement.

Abstract describes a theoretical/teaching physiology paper about body fluid composition, not an empirical task-based fMRI study. It reports no human subjects performing problem-solving tasks or any task-related brain activation. Excluded as a non-fMRI study and lacking a healthy adult task sample.

Abstract describes a task-based fMRI study of fluid reasoning in humans and reports task-related BOLD activation results. Age/health of participants not specified in abstract, so include for full-text review to confirm presence of healthy adults (18-65).
Study tested children aged 7–9 (no healthy adult sample) and describes behavioral training with standard cognitive tests—no task-based fMRI reported. Fails adult-sample and fMRI inclusion criteria.

The study uses diffusion tensor imaging (DTI), not task-based fMRI, and focuses on adolescents (math-gifted and controls), not healthy adults (18–65). It also reports structural white-matter connectivity rather than task-related fMRI activation.

Study is basic biomedical research on aquaporin gene delivery in mice/kidney, not an fMRI study of human problem-solving. It does not involve task-based fMRI, human adult participants, or report brain activation. Exclusion criteria E4 (non-fMRI) and E3 (no healthy adult sample) apply.

Participants are children (ages 8-12); the study does not include healthy adults (18-65). Although it is a task-based fMRI study of reasoning and reports activation, it fails the requirement for an adult healthy sample, so it is excluded.

This is a behavioral genetic study of 12-year-old twins using a cognitive test battery (VESPARCH 1). It is not a task-based fMRI study, does not report brain activation results, and does not include adult participants (18–65).
Abstract describes a behavioral latent-variable study of working memory and reasoning with no mention of fMRI or any neuroimaging; therefore it is a non-fMRI study and does not meet the review scope.
Abstract describes behavioral working-memory training and transfer effects; no mention of fMRI, neuroimaging, or task-related brain activation. Therefore it is not a task-based fMRI study.

Task-based fMRI study with participants performing analogy/semantic problem-solving. Includes a young adult group alongside children (healthy participants) and reports task-related brain activation and age-related changes. Fits all inclusion criteria.

This article is a radiology checklist/review about HRCT lung abnormalities, not an empirical task-based fMRI study of problem solving in healthy adults. It is a non-fMRI review/guideline, so it meets exclusion criteria E4 (non-fMRI) and E1 (review).

The abstract describes a behavioral/psychometric study of a relational integration task and its prediction of fluid reasoning; there is no mention of fMRI data collection or task-related brain activation. Therefore it is a non-fMRI study and does not meet the review inclusion requirements.

This is a historical/theoretical review of concepts of mind, not an empirical task-based fMRI study with participants or reported task-related brain activation. It meets exclusion criteria: review/article type and non-fMRI study.

This is a commentary/reanalysis/meta-analytic reanalysis of behavioral latent-variable studies on working memory and intelligence, not a task-based fMRI experiment. The abstract does not report any fMRI data or task-related brain activation. Matches exclusion criteria for review/meta-analysis (E1) and non-fMRI study (E4).

The sample consists of healthy participants aged 8–19 (children/adolescents); no adult (18–65) group is reported, so the study fails inclusion criterion I3 requiring at least one healthy adult group. Although it is a task-based fMRI reporting activations, the absence of an adult sample justifies exclusion based on E3.

This is a commentary/reanalysis/meta-analytic article on working memory and intelligence, not an empirical task-based fMRI study. It is a review/comment (E1) and not an fMRI study (E4). Does not report task-related brain activation in healthy adults.

This is a narrative review about artificial nutrition/hydration for terminally ill patients, not an empirical task-based fMRI study of problem solving in healthy adults. It does not report fMRI task activation or include healthy adult participants.
Not a task-based fMRI study; it's a clinical review on fluid management in pre-eclampsia, with no healthy adult problem-solving fMRI data reported.

Study involves children (not healthy adults) and is behavioral/neuropsychological; abstract gives no indication of task-based fMRI. Fails inclusion criteria I1 and I3 (thus meets exclusions E4 and E3).

The study correlates Raven scores with regional gray matter volume and regional homogeneity (ReHo) across participants, reflecting structural and resting-state/functional connectivity analyses rather than a task-based fMRI experiment measuring task-related activation during problem solving. The abstract does not report task-evoked activation. Exclude based on resting-state/connectivity-only design.

This is a literature review about clinically assisted hydration in dying patients, not an original task-based fMRI study. It does not involve task-based fMRI, problem-solving tasks, or healthy adult fMRI participants.
Abstract describes behavioral experiments (modified n-back) with no mention of fMRI or brain activation results. This is a non-fMRI study, so it does not meet the task-based fMRI requirement.

The study is a theoretical/biological morphogenesis paper about branching organs in tissues, not a task-based fMRI study nor involving human adult participants. It does not report task-related brain activation.
Study participants are adolescents (math-gifted and controls); no healthy adult (18–65) sample is reported, so it fails the requirement for inclusion of adults.

The abstract describes a clinical/review article about lymphedema and wound compression therapy, not a task-based fMRI study. It does not involve problem-solving tasks or report fMRI activation in healthy adults. Exclusion criteria E4 (non-fMRI) and E1 (review/meta-analysis) apply.

This is a theoretical/modeling paper on communicating hydrocephalus in a pediatric neurosurgery context, not a task-based fMRI study of problem solving. It does not report fMRI data or healthy adult participants. Therefore it does not meet inclusion criteria.

The article is a nephrology review/opinion piece about peritoneal dialysis and residual renal function, not an fMRI study. It does not involve healthy adult participants or report task-related brain activation. Exclusion criteria E1, E3, and E4 apply.

Task-based fMRI study examining nonverbal reasoning in humans, reports frontoparietal task-related activations and dissociable prefrontal subregions. Appears to involve healthy adult participants and directly assesses problem-solving/reasoning.

This is a narrative review of cognitive/behavioral functioning in individuals with Costello syndrome (clinical population). It is not a task-based fMRI study, includes no healthy adult sample, and reports no task-related brain activation.
Study used structural MRI and cognitive assessments, not task-based fMRI; it does not report task-related brain activation. Therefore it does not meet the fMRI requirement for inclusion.

This is a narrative review on sex differences in intelligence and educational implications; it does not report original task-based fMRI data, participants performing problem-solving tasks, or task-related brain activation. It therefore meets exclusion criteria for reviews (E1) and non-fMRI studies (E4).

This is a task-based fMRI study in human participants performing the Raven's Progressive Matrices (a problem-solving/reasoning task). It reports task-related brain activation in seven healthy young adults, satisfying all inclusion criteria.
This is a laboratory study of myometrial tissue and lactate transport in pregnant women, not a task-based fMRI study of problem solving. It does not report fMRI task-related brain activation.

The study used transcranial alternating current stimulation and behavioral testing (Raven’s matrices); no task-based fMRI or brain activation imaging is reported in the abstract. Therefore it is a non-fMRI study and outside the review scope.

This is a task-based fMRI study where participants performed Raven's matrices (a reasoning/problem-solving task). It includes a non-autistic healthy adult control group and reports task-related activations (extrastriate BA18, lateral prefrontal BA9, parietal BA7). All inclusion criteria are clearly met and no exclusion criteria apply.

Abstract describes psychometric evaluation of cognitive and selection tests in junior doctors; no mention of fMRI data acquisition or task-related brain activation. This is a non-fMRI study and does not meet the task-based fMRI requirement.

Abstract describes a behavioral study of general knowledge and reasoning with no mention of fMRI, neuroimaging, or brain activation results. Therefore it is a non-fMRI study and does not meet the review’s task-based fMRI requirement.
Abstract describes a behavioral study of game performance and cognitive tasks with no mention of fMRI or brain imaging. Not a task-based fMRI study, so excluded.

The abstract describes an ethical/legal discussion of artificial nutrition in elderly patients, not a task-based fMRI study of problem solving. It does not involve healthy adult participants performing problem-solving tasks nor report any brain activation results.

The study is a longitudinal behavioral assessment of children/adolescents (ages ~12–15), not adults, and does not report any fMRI data. Therefore it fails inclusion criteria for an adult healthy fMRI task-based sample.

Abstract describes behavioral measures and factor analysis of executive functions and fluid reasoning with no mention of fMRI, task-based scanning, or task-related brain activation. Therefore this is a non-fMRI study and should be excluded.

This is a behavioral psychometric study reporting correlations between cognitive tests in college students; no fMRI or brain activation methods/results are described, so it does not meet the task-based fMRI requirement.

Abstract describes neurodevelopmental outcomes after pediatric cardiac surgery (infants/children); it is not a task-based fMRI study, involves no healthy adult (18–65) sample, and does not report task-related fMRI activations. Does not meet inclusion criteria I1–I4 and clearly meets exclusions E3 and E4.

This is a historical/educational review of kidney knowledge in the Enlightenment, not an empirical task-based fMRI study of problem solving in healthy adults. It does not report fMRI task-related brain activation results and is a review article.

The abstract describes a qualitative policy analysis of NICE and governance, not a task-based fMRI experiment. It does not involve fMRI, problem-solving tasks, or reporting task-related brain activation in healthy adults. Excluded based on non-fMRI study.

Abstract describes computerized clinical protocols and patient management (mechanical ventilation/ARDS), not a task-based fMRI study. No healthy adult sample is reported. Does not report fMRI results or problem-solving tasks.

This is an educational/review-style article about mathematics in microbiology education, not a task-based fMRI study. It reports no human fMRI data, no problem-solving task in healthy adults, and provides no task-related brain activation results.

This is a behavioral controlled study of MDMA effects on cognition with no mention of fMRI or task-based neuroimaging. It does not meet the task-based fMRI inclusion criterion (I1) and therefore should be excluded as a non-fMRI study (E4).

Abstract describes behavioral/neuropsychological testing (Block Design, Matrix Reasoning, reaction time, WCST, Tower of London) with no mention of fMRI, neuroimaging, or task-related brain activation. Therefore this is a non-fMRI study and must be excluded.

This is an open trial of an attention-training intervention in school-aged children with ADHD. It does not involve task-based fMRI, does not include healthy adult participants (18–65), and reports behavioral/clinical outcomes rather than task-related brain activation relevant to problem-solving fMRI in healthy adults.

This is a behavioral longitudinal cognitive assessment in patients with Costello syndrome; the abstract reports no fMRI data and contains no healthy adult sample. Fails inclusion criteria I1 and I3. Excluded based on Non-fMRI study and no healthy adult participants.

The study is not an fMRI study (behavioral sensory/cognitive testing) and the healthy control group consists of older adults (mean age ~76), so it does not include healthy adults aged 18–65. Therefore E4 and E3 apply.

This is a nutrition intervention trial in Indian schoolchildren assessing growth and cognitive outcomes; it is not a task-based fMRI study and does not include healthy adult participants (ages 18-65).

This is an educational survey of undergraduate public/global health offerings, not an fMRI experiment. It contains no task-based neuroimaging, no participants performing problem-solving tasks, and reports no brain activation results. Therefore it fails the requirement for fMRI data.

Abstract describes behavioral task-switching and cognitive measures without any mention of fMRI, neuroimaging, or brain activation; therefore it is a non-fMRI study and does not meet the review objective.

Abstract reads like a commentary on a neuroimaging study rather than reporting original task-based fMRI methods, participants, or results. Published in Trends in Cognitive Sciences (a review/commentary journal); no details on healthy adult sample or task-based activation are provided. Exclude as a review/commentary.

This is a longitudinal behavioral study of cognitive performance in older adults (HOPE); not a task-based fMRI study and participants are older than the target 18–65 healthy adult range. Does not report task-related brain activation.

Task-based fMRI study in healthy adult sample (28 healthy males) reporting task-related BOLD activations during a reversal learning task tied to fluid intelligence. Reversal learning assesses flexible problem solving/reasoning and the abstract explicitly links prediction-error signaling to problem solving capacity, so it meets inclusion criteria; full text review warranted to confirm task qualifies as problem-solving.

Task-based fMRI study of fluid reasoning and perceptual speed in humans (N=106, ages 20–77). Reports task-related activation patterns linking neural topography to behavioral performance, meets all inclusion criteria.
Abstract reports behavioral psychometric testing in children (Grades 1–5) and does not mention fMRI or brain activation. Does not include healthy adult participants and is not an fMRI study.

Abstract reports a web-based cognitive assessment study (NCPT) without any mention of fMRI, brain activation, or neuroimaging. It is a non-fMRI behavioral/psychometric study and therefore does not meet the task-based fMRI requirement.

The study is behavioral with adolescent participants (not healthy adults 18-65) and does not involve fMRI or report task-related brain activation. Therefore it fails the review's population and imaging modality requirements.
This is a behavioural sleep study assessing anagram problem solving after REM/NREM awakenings; it does not use task-based fMRI. Therefore it meets exclusion criterion E4 (Non-fMRI study).

The abstract describes mapping β-amyloid burden and its relation to cognition in healthy aging; it does not report any task-based fMRI, problem-solving or reasoning tasks, or task-related brain activation. The study appears to use non-fMRI amyloid imaging, so it does not meet the fMRI requirement.

Task-based fMRI study using Raven's Progressive Matrices; participants include autistic and non-autistic (healthy) adults performing a reasoning/problem-solving task. Abstract reports task-related brain activation and connectivity results related to task complexity.

The study is not an fMRI experiment (neuropsychological/behavioral assessment) and participants are clinical (current depression and hazardous alcohol use), so it lacks healthy adult fMRI task data. Therefore it meets exclusion criteria E4 and E3.

This is an animal (rat) physiological study of splenic denervation and endotoxemia; it is not an fMRI study and includes no human participants. Excluded for non-fMRI methodology and absence of healthy adult human sample.

This is a structural (anatomical) MRI longitudinal study using fractal dimension measures, not a task-based fMRI study of problem solving. Participants are older adults (~68–73), outside the 18–65 healthy adult range. Therefore it does not meet fMRI task criteria and does not include the target age group.

Abstract describes a behavioral study of reasoning about social conflicts with no mention of fMRI acquisition or task-related brain activation results; therefore it is a non-fMRI study and does not meet inclusion criterion I1. Exclusion E4 applies.

This is a resting-state pharmacological fMRI study examining intrinsic functional connectivity; no task-based problem-solving or task-activation results are reported. Therefore it meets exclusion criterion E2 (resting-state/connectivity-only).

This is a task-based fMRI study where participants performed a modified Raven's Progressive Matrices (a problem-solving/reasoning task). It includes a healthy adult control group (matched NC) and reports task-related brain activation results. Thus it satisfies all inclusion criteria.

This study is about bevacizumab treatment for radiation necrosis, not a task-based fMRI study of problem solving. It does not involve healthy adult participants performing reasoning tasks nor report task-related fMRI activation. Excluded for being a non-fMRI clinical treatment study and for lacking a healthy adult sample.

This is a longitudinal behavioral study of children/adolescents (early elementary to age 15) examining cognitive predictors of reasoning; it does not use task-based fMRI and does not include healthy adult participants (18–65).

Abstract reports a behavioral study of social support and cognitive performance in older African American adults; no mention of fMRI or task-based brain activation, and sample mean age (69) indicates participants are outside the healthy adult 18–65 range. Does not meet meta-analysis requirements.

Task-based fMRI study using Raven-style reasoning tasks in human participants, reports task-related cortical activation differences between high- and low-IQ adult groups—meets inclusion criteria and no exclusion criteria are evident from the abstract.

This is a behavioral/psychophysiology study of emotion regulation (reappraisal) with measures like self-report, EMG, heart rate, and EDA—no mention of fMRI. The task is not a problem-solving/reasoning task. Therefore it does not meet the fMRI requirement.

Abstract reports a genetic association study using cognitive tests and structural equation modeling; no task-based fMRI was conducted or reported. Therefore it does not meet the fMRI requirement for inclusion.

Abstract describes behavioral assessments (Navon task, Remote Associates, Alternate Uses, Raven) with no mention of fMRI or any neuroimaging; therefore this is a non-fMRI study and does not report task-related brain activation.

Abstract describes behavioral experiments on analogy generation and transfer; no mention of fMRI, neuroimaging, or task-related brain activation. Study appears non-fMRI, so it does not meet the fMRI requirement.

This study reports structural MRI and 1H-MR spectroscopy in patient groups (MIDD and T1D), not a task-based fMRI study of problem-solving in healthy adults. There is no healthy adult (18–65) sample performing a reasoning task and no task-related fMRI activation reported.

Task-based fMRI study in healthy adults (ages 20–80) performing cognitive tasks including fluid reasoning/problem-solving; reports task-related brain activation (GLM, activation topographies, component prediction). Satisfies all inclusion criteria I1–I4.

This study reports neuropsychological assessment (NEPSY) in children (age 6–12) with spina bifida, not task-based fMRI, and does not include healthy adult participants. It therefore does not meet the fMRI or healthy-adult inclusion criteria.

This study used T1-weighted structural MRI (cortical thickness and subcortical volumes) and reports relationships between gray matter and cognition; it does not report task-based fMRI or task-related brain activation during a problem-solving/reasoning task. Therefore it is a non-fMRI study for this review.

The abstract describes a longitudinal behavioral cognitive study in older adults (ages 64–92) with no mention of fMRI or brain activation results. It is therefore not a task-based fMRI study (fails I1/I4) and does not meet the review imaging requirement.

Study is a behavioral psychometric investigation in children (ages 6–12) using DAS and K-ABC; no task-based fMRI was reported and the sample are not healthy adults (18–65). Does not meet inclusion criteria.
Behavioral developmental study of children (ages 4–7) using psychometric tests; no fMRI methods, no task-related brain activation reported, and no adult (18–65) sample.

Behavioral study of children with Asperger's using Raven's matrices; no fMRI was reported and participants are children with a clinical diagnosis rather than healthy adults. Fails inclusion criteria for adult healthy sample and fMRI modality.

This is an animal (mouse) study focusing on nasal turbinate development and airflow; it is not a human task-based fMRI study of problem solving. It includes no healthy adult human participants and reports morphological and CFD results rather than task-related brain activation.

The study is a human physiology experiment assessing hydration, temperature, mood, cognition and balance without any mention of fMRI or neuroimaging data. It does not meet the task-based fMRI requirement, so exclude as a non-fMRI study.

Study examines sleep, cognitive impairment, and depression in maintenance hemodialysis patients; it is not a task-based fMRI study and does not include healthy adult participants. Abstract gives no indication of fMRI, problem-solving tasks, or healthy controls.

Abstract describes psychometric structural modeling of emotional intelligence in a behavioral sample; no mention of fMRI, neuroimaging, or brain activation. Therefore it is not a task-based fMRI study and fails inclusion criterion I1 (Non-fMRI study).

This is a behavioral psychometric study (Everyday Problem Solving Test) in older African-American adults — not a task-based fMRI study. The sample is older (mean age 67.8), outside the 18–65 healthy adult range. Exclusion is clear from abstract.
The sample consists of high school students (adolescents), not adults aged 18–65, so the study lacks a healthy adult sample required for inclusion.

Abstract reports behavioral emotion-recognition studies in elderly adults with no mention of fMRI/neuroimaging and tasks are not problem-solving/reasoning. Participants are elderly (outside 18–65). Fails inclusion criteria; exclude based on E4 and E3.

This is a conceptual/comparative review of working memory evolution, not an original task-based fMRI experiment. The abstract describes synthesis of comparative literature and does not report task-based fMRI data, human participants performing problem-solving tasks, or task-related activation results.

The study is not a task-based fMRI study and does not report brain activation; it focuses on dietetic diagnostic codes in hemodialysis patients (clinical population), not healthy adults. Therefore it fails inclusion and meets exclusion criteria E4 (non-fMRI study) and E3 (no healthy adult sample).

Abstract describes behavioral/psychometric testing of cognition and activity engagement in older adults; no mention of fMRI or task-related brain activation. Therefore it is not a task-based fMRI study.

The study involves healthy children and investigates sleep (CAP) and cognitive performance—likely using sleep EEG/polysomnography—not a task-based fMRI in adults performing problem-solving. Therefore it lacks a healthy adult sample (E3) and is a non-fMRI study (E4).

This is a cross-sectional behavioral study of intellectual abilities in older adults (ages 70–103). The abstract does not report any fMRI data or task-related brain activation, and the sample is outside the target 18–65 healthy adult range. Not relevant to task-based fMRI problem-solving in healthy adults.

This is an animal (Dahl salt-sensitive rats) physiology study of hydrochlorothiazide effects on renal/interstitial calcium; it is not an fMRI study, does not involve human adults performing problem-solving, and does not report task-related brain activation.

This is an animal (rat) endocrine study examining HPA responses to LPS/IL-1β, not a human task-based fMRI study of problem solving. It does not include healthy adult human participants and is not an fMRI study, so it fails the review’s inclusion criteria.

Abstract discusses assessment of cognitive abilities in the elderly and dementia, with no mention of task-based fMRI or problem-solving tasks. Participants are elderly (outside 18–65 target range) and no fMRI methods are reported. Therefore it does not meet the review’s inclusion criteria.

Abstract describes longitudinal behavioral cognitive testing (WJ-R) and latent growth models; no mention of fMRI, brain activation, or task-based neuroimaging. Does not meet task-based fMRI requirement.

This is a methodological review/chapter about computational methods in biochemical and bioinformatics fields, not an empirical task-based fMRI study with healthy adult participants. It does not report task-related brain activation. Exclusion criteria E1 (review) and E4 (non-fMRI) apply.
Study is a genetic/behavioral study in elderly participants using cognitive tests and structural MRI; it is not a task-based fMRI study and the sample is older than the 18–65 adult range required.

This is a task-based fMRI study in 225 healthy young adults reporting BOLD activation during reasoning (problem-solving) related to fluid and crystallized intelligence, satisfying all inclusion criteria.

This is a longitudinal behavioral study of spoken discourse memory in older adults (ACTIVE trial) reporting cognitive test-based analyses. It does not use task-based fMRI or report task-related brain activation, so it does not meet the fMRI inclusion criteria.

Not a task-based fMRI study: it reports biomarker analyses in Alzheimer’s disease using postmortem brain, ventricular fluid, and CSF. No task-based functional MRI, no healthy adult task sample, and no task-related brain activation results are reported.

The abstract describes a behavioral working-memory training study assessing recognition memory and fluid intelligence with no mention of fMRI data, task-based neuroimaging, or brain activation results. It is therefore a non-fMRI study and meets exclusion criterion E4.

Abstract describes a medical education simulation trial with senior medical students preparing for clinical practice; it does not report any task-based fMRI methods or brain activation results. Therefore it is not an fMRI study and does not meet the review objective.
This is a behavioral longitudinal study of personality, activity, and cognitive performance in older adults; no task-based fMRI or any fMRI methods are reported, so it does not meet imaging criteria.

The study is a behavioral neuropsychological follow-up in boys (children) with clinical diagnoses; no task-based fMRI methods or task-related brain activation are reported, and there is no healthy adult sample. Excluded for non-fMRI study and lack of healthy adult participants.
Abstract reports behavioral study on working memory, pressure, and fluid intelligence with no mention of fMRI methods or task-related brain activation; therefore it is a non-fMRI study.

Abstract describes a behavioral cognitive training randomized trial with no mention of fMRI methods or task-related brain activation results. This is a non-fMRI study and does not report neuroimaging outcomes relevant to the fMRI problem-solving meta-analysis.

Task-based fMRI study where healthy participants performed Raven's Progressive Matrices (a problem-solving/reasoning task) and task-related brain activation/connectivity during the task is reported. Age range not specified in abstract, so full text needed to confirm 18-65, but other inclusion criteria are clearly met.

Abstract describes a virtual reality behavioral study of learning spatial intuition; it does not mention fMRI data or brain activation. Therefore it is not a task-based fMRI study and should be excluded.

Abstract describes a behavioral study of theory-of-mind with no mention of fMRI, neuroimaging methods, or task-related brain activation results. Therefore it is a non-fMRI study and does not meet the meta-analysis imaging requirement.

This is a birth-cohort behavioral study of 9–10-year-old children assessing birthweight and cognitive tests; it is not a task-based fMRI study and does not include healthy adult participants. Excluded for non-fMRI study (E4) and no healthy adult sample (E3).
Participants are adolescents (mean age 16.5), so the study does not include a healthy adult sample (18–65), which conflicts with the review’s adult-sample requirement.

This is a lesion neuropsychology study of patients with prefrontal (and other) brain damage using Matrix Reasoning; no task-based fMRI is reported and the sample comprises brain-damaged patients rather than healthy adults. Excluded for non-fMRI methodology and lack of healthy adult sample.

This is a computational/formal-methods paper about anatomical/physiological modeling, not a task-based fMRI study. It does not involve human participants performing problem-solving or report task-related brain activation.

The study describes a wastewater treatment plant monitoring method; it is an engineering paper with no fMRI data or human participants performing problem-solving tasks. Not relevant to task-based fMRI in healthy adults.

This is a clinical case report of an infectious illness; it does not involve any fMRI methods or healthy adult participants performing problem-solving tasks. Therefore it does not meet the review’s requirements.
The study sampled children (4th–5th grade), not adults, and the abstract describes behavioral/psychometric assessments with no mention of fMRI. Does not meet adult sample or fMRI criteria.

This study reports behavioral pitch and melody memory in school-aged children with autism; the abstract does not describe any fMRI methods or include healthy adult participants. It therefore does not meet the review's focus on task-based fMRI in healthy adults.

This is a diffusion tensor MRI (DT-MRI) structural study of white matter in middle-aged/older adults, not a task-based fMRI study of problem solving and it does not report task-related fMRI activation. Therefore it is a non-fMRI study and excluded.

This is a behavioral study of crossword puzzle proficiency and age-cognition relations with no mention of fMRI, neuroimaging, or task-related brain activation. It does not meet the review’s requirement for task-based fMRI, so exclude.

Study is not an fMRI study (measures salivary cortisol, blood pressure, heart rate) and participants are elderly (65-80), so no healthy adult sample aged 18-65. Does not meet key inclusion criteria for task-based fMRI in healthy adults.
Study is behavioral and psychometric in GO players; no mention of fMRI or neuroimaging. Does not meet task-based fMRI requirement.

Abstract describes a behavioral neuropsychological study (Tower of London, WAIS-III) with no mention of fMRI or task-related brain activation; therefore it is a non-fMRI study and does not meet the review objective.

This is a single-patient clinical case report about neuroborreliosis in an elderly man. It is not an fMRI study, does not involve a problem-solving task, and includes no healthy adult sample. Therefore it meets exclusion criteria E4 and E3.
Event-related task-based fMRI study with 12 adult participants performing fluid analogy/problem-solving tasks; reports task-related BOLD activations and ROI correlations. Fits all inclusion criteria.
This is a qualitative social study of commuting, not a task-based fMRI investigation of problem solving. No neuroimaging or fMRI methods are reported, so it does not meet the meta-analysis objective.

Study is not an fMRI study and does not include healthy adult participants performing problem-solving tasks; it examines cognitive test performance in elderly clinical groups (Alzheimer’s and depression).

The task is a time-estimation task manipulating feedback and social context, not a problem-solving or reasoning task required by the review (does not meet I2). The study is task-based fMRI and reports task-related activations (I1, I4), but it does not investigate problem solving in healthy adults based on the abstract.

This is a historical/anatomical analysis of William Hunter's bone specimens, not an empirical task-based fMRI study of problem solving in humans. It includes no healthy adult participants or fMRI activation results.

The fMRI task is a memory encoding task, not a problem-solving or reasoning task required by inclusion criterion I2; therefore the study does not address the review objective. It does meet I1, I3, and I4 but fails I2.

Abstract reports peripheral microvascular physiology (venous congestion and tilt in human legs) with no mention of fMRI, brain imaging, or any problem-solving cognitive task. Does not meet the review’s focus on task-based fMRI in healthy adults.

This is a clinical case report of a single patient with neurosyphilis/ocular disease. It is not a task-based fMRI study, does not involve healthy adult participants performing problem-solving tasks, and reports clinical diagnosis and treatment rather than task-related brain activation. Excluded on those bases.
This is a molecular/immunology study using latently infected B cell lines; it does not use task-based fMRI, does not include healthy adult human participants, and reports no brain activation results.

Animal study in mice investigating lung regeneration (pneumonectomy), not a task-based fMRI study of problem solving in humans and does not report brain activation. Exclude due to non-human sample and non-fMRI methods.

This is an engineering paper on visualization and decision support for decentralized wastewater treatment; it does not report any fMRI data or human participants performing problem-solving tasks, and contains no task-related brain activation results. Therefore it is a non-fMRI study with no healthy adult sample.
Abstract describes an online randomized cognitive training trial with no mention of fMRI, neuroimaging, or task-based brain activation results. Therefore it is not a task-based fMRI study.

This paper describes a mathematical model of thrombin generation and CFD-suitable equations; it is not an fMRI study, includes no human problem-solving tasks or healthy adult participants, and reports no task-related brain activation results.

Abstract describes behavioral investigation of working memory and fluid intelligence with no mention of fMRI, brain imaging, or task-related activation data. Therefore it is a non-fMRI study and does not meet inclusion.

This is a behavioral study of typically developing children (48–77 months) using cognitive tests; it does not include healthy adult participants and does not use task-based fMRI, thus meeting exclusion criteria E3 and E4.

The study used diffusion tensor imaging (DTI) to assess white matter changes after reasoning training, not task-based fMRI. It therefore does not meet the meta-analysis requirement for fMRI studies (Exclusion criterion E4: Non-fMRI studies).

This study tests medication effects on reading in elementary school children with ADHD; it is not an fMRI study and does not include healthy adult participants. Therefore it does not meet task-based fMRI nor healthy adult sample criteria.

Abstract describes development of abbreviated Raven's matrices (psychometric test); no task-based fMRI, no brain activation results reported. This is a non-fMRI test development study and thus outside the review scope.

The article describes an engineering/AI system for wastewater treatment supervision, not a task-based fMRI study. It has no human participants performing problem-solving tasks and reports no brain activation results.

This is an immunology study of cerebrospinal fluid T cells, not a task-based fMRI investigation of problem solving in healthy adults. It does not involve fMRI, task-based problem solving, or report brain activation results.

This is a virology/cell biology study of rhinovirus and airway epithelial responses, not a task-based fMRI study of problem solving in healthy adults. It includes no fMRI methods or healthy adult sample. Exclude per E4 and E3.
Qualitative nursing study using interviews and think-aloud; no mention of fMRI or any neuroimaging. Does not meet task-based fMRI requirement, so exclude as a non-fMRI study.

This is an animal physiology study (shark rectal gland) investigating membrane permeabilities, not a human task-based fMRI study of problem solving. It does not involve human adult participants or fMRI methods, so it fails inclusion criteria.

Study is clinical research on individuals with Costello syndrome using standardized cognitive testing; it is not a task-based fMRI study nor does it include a healthy adult sample. Exclude for these reasons.

Abstract describes a behavioral study with secondary school students (not adults) and reports no fMRI or brain activation results. Therefore it is a non-fMRI study and does not include a healthy adult sample.

The study is a longitudinal behavioral/neurodevelopmental assessment in adolescents with fragile X syndrome (ages 10-16) using the Leiter-R; it is not a task-based fMRI study and does not include healthy adult participants. Therefore it fails the fMRI and healthy adult criteria.

This is a proteomic/transcriptome study of synovial fluid and joint tissues in osteoarthritis, not a task-based fMRI study of problem solving in healthy adults. It is a non-fMRI study, so excluded (E4).

The study is an observational longitudinal behavioral study and does not report any fMRI data or task-related brain activation. It therefore does not meet the requirement of being a task-based fMRI study.
Participants are young deaf children (ages 2–5), not healthy adults, and the study reports behavioral/cognitive assessments following cochlear implantation with no task-based fMRI methods reported.

This is an animal (guinea pig) study of bacterial virulence and lung permeability, not a task-based fMRI study of problem solving in humans. It involves no healthy adult participants and does not use fMRI methods.

This is a review article in colloid/interface physics about layering and prewetting, not a task-based fMRI study with human participants. It is a review (E1), involves non-fMRI physical experiments (E4), and does not include healthy adult human participants (E3).

Task-based fMRI comparing two fluid reasoning tasks (Visualization, Induction) with task-specific neural coupling results and brain–behavior prediction. Abstract does not specify participant ages/health, so include for full-text review to confirm healthy adult sample and detailed activation reporting.

This is a microbiological study of dialysis fluids comparing culture media; it is not a task-based fMRI study and does not involve human problem-solving tasks or healthy adult fMRI data, so it does not meet the review objective.
Behavioral longitudinal study of older adults (ages ~70-79); not a task-based fMRI study and does not include healthy adults aged 18-65. No task-related brain activation results reported.

Abstract describes behavioral experiments on transitive inference and reports reasoning accuracy and confidence but makes no mention of fMRI, neuroimaging, or task-related brain activation. Therefore it is a non-fMRI study.
Preclinical animal (rat) study using optical imaging; not an fMRI study and includes no human healthy adult participants. Does not meet task-based fMRI or healthy adult criteria.

The study used structural MRI and genetic/behavioral analyses (hippocampal volume associations) rather than task-based fMRI and does not report task-related brain activation or fMRI task results. Therefore it is a non-fMRI study and does not meet the review objective.

This is a developmental behavioral study of autobiographical life narratives with children and young adults; no fMRI or neuroimaging methods or task-based brain activation results are reported, so it does not meet the fMRI requirement.

This is a clinical study of palliative care for fungating malignant wounds in patients; it is not a task-based fMRI study, does not involve problem-solving tasks or healthy adult participants, and reports no task-related brain activation.

This is an animal (rat) hemorrhage resuscitation study, not a task-based fMRI study and does not include healthy adult human participants performing problem-solving tasks. Thus it meets exclusion criteria for non-fMRI and no healthy adult sample.

The abstract describes analytical and simulation work and behavioral/psychometric testing of working memory and fluid intelligence; it contains no mention of fMRI, brain imaging, or task-related activation results. Therefore it is a non-fMRI study and should be excluded.

This is an engineering paper about an expert system for anaerobic digestion plants, not a task-based fMRI study with human participants performing problem-solving tasks. It describes a diagnostic knowledge-base and experiments on an industrial digester, not neuroimaging of healthy adults. Exclusion criteria E4 (non-fMRI) and E3 (no healthy adult sample) apply.

This is an engineering demonstration of a space ECLSS; it is not an fMRI study and contains no human participants performing problem-solving or reasoning tasks. It does not report task-related brain activation.

The abstract describes lessons from fieldwork evaluating an HIV prevention intervention with adolescents; it is not an fMRI study and does not include healthy adults (18-65). Exclude for non-fMRI methodology and non-adult sample.

This is an epidemiological study of Toxoplasma infection risk in slaughterhouse workers; the abstract contains no indication of fMRI, task-based problem-solving paradigms, or relevant neuroimaging outcomes. Therefore it does not meet the task-based fMRI requirement and should be excluded as a non-fMRI study.

The study describes an engineering decision-support system for activated sludge (wastewater) operations, not a task-based fMRI study. It involves no human participants or neuroimaging data. Therefore it does not meet the review’s fMRI or healthy adult participant criteria.

Abstract describes psychometric factor analysis of cognitive task scores; no mention of fMRI, neuroimaging, scanner procedures, or task-related brain activation. This is a non-fMRI study and therefore does not meet the review’s neuroimaging inclusion criteria.

This is an animal (Xenopus) developmental biology study investigating connexin26 in embryos; it is not a task-based fMRI study and involves no human adult participants. Therefore it does not meet the review criteria.

This is a clinical case report of a single patient with heart failure and hyponatraemia; it is not an fMRI study, does not include healthy adult participants performing a problem-solving task, and reports clinical management rather than task-related brain activation.

Not an fMRI study or a problem-solving task; the abstract reports immunology research on TSLP and macrophage polarization in allergic inflammation (human/mouse), with no healthy adult task-based fMRI data.

The study tests task-switching training in children with ADHD (clinical pediatric sample) and reports behavioral outcomes, not task-based fMRI results. Therefore it lacks a healthy adult fMRI sample and is non-fMRI.

This is an obstetric cohort study predicting neonatal metabolic acidosis in pregnant women; it does not involve task-based fMRI, problem-solving or reasoning tasks, nor report brain activation results, and the sample are pregnant women (not healthy adult problem-solving participants). Excluded per E4 (non-fMRI study) and E3 (no healthy adult sample).
This is a behavioral cognitive-aging study of a substitution coding task with no mention of fMRI or any neuroimaging. It does not meet the task-based fMRI requirement, so exclude as a non-fMRI study.

This study is not a task-based fMRI study and does not include adult participants. It analyzes PIAT-M scores in children from NLSY to examine the Flynn Effect across subgroups, so it fails the review inclusion criteria. Exclusion criteria E3 (no healthy adult sample) and E4 (non-fMRI study) apply.
This is an animal (mouse) immunology study of NK cells and pulmonary fibrosis, not a human task-based fMRI study of problem solving. It does not involve healthy adult humans or fMRI methods.

The abstract describes a behavioral latent-variable study of working memory and reasoning without any mention of fMRI methods or task-related brain activation; it is therefore a non-fMRI study and does not meet the review's imaging requirement.

The study used behavioral standardized tests (Woodcock-Johnson) rather than task-based fMRI, and the sample consisted of college students with diagnosed learning disabilities (no healthy adult group). Therefore E4 (non-fMRI) and E3 (no healthy adults) apply.

Not a task-based fMRI study and participants are children rather than healthy adults. Abstract describes neuropsychological testing (non-fMRI) in child clinical groups and child controls, so it fails inclusion of healthy adult sample and is non-fMRI.

Abstract describes peptide targeting to choroid plexus for CNS drug delivery — a preclinical/biological study, not a task-based fMRI study in healthy adults. No human problem-solving task or fMRI results reported.

This is an in vivo pharmacology/microdialysis study in rats examining serotonergic autoreceptors and SSRI effects. It is not a task-based fMRI study, does not involve human participants performing problem-solving tasks, and does not report task-related brain activation.

Abstract describes a computational model and simulations with behavioral data; it does not report task-based fMRI methods or task-related brain activation. Therefore it is a non-fMRI study and does not meet the review’s fMRI requirement.

The study is an ergonomics case study of emergency medical dispatch and distributed cognition; it is not an fMRI study and reports no task-related brain activation. It does not meet the task-based neuroimaging inclusion criteria.
Preclinical cellular study in rabbits; not a human task-based fMRI study and provides no task-related brain activation data. Does not meet inclusion criteria I1–I4.
This is an immunology/oncology study of tumor-infiltrating CD8+ T cells in ovarian cancer. It does not involve task-based fMRI, problem-solving tasks, or a healthy adult sample.

This is a historical/narrative review about membrane molecular biology, not an original task-based fMRI study of problem solving. No human participants, tasks, or fMRI results are reported. Excluded as review (E1), non-fMRI (E4), and no healthy adult sample (E3).

This is a developmental psychology study analyzing life narratives via text-analytic methods and interviews; there is no mention of fMRI or neuroimaging and the tasks are autobiographical narratives rather than problem-solving. Therefore it is a non-fMRI study and does not meet the review objective.

Abstract describes a longitudinal behavioral study of intelligence across ages without any mention of fMRI, task-based neuroimaging, or brain activation results. Therefore it is a non-fMRI study and does not meet inclusion criteria.

This is an animal (mouse) study testing a drug for polycystic kidney disease, not a human task-based fMRI study of problem solving. No healthy adult participants or fMRI activation results are reported.

Study is an observational nutrition study in children aged 6–10, not a task-based fMRI study in healthy adults. It does not involve fMRI or adult participants performing problem-solving or reasoning tasks.

Study involves patients with mild cognitive impairment, focuses on structural morphometry and CSF biomarkers and episodic memory, not task-based fMRI or problem-solving in healthy adults. No healthy adult sample and not an fMRI task study.

This is a case study of cognitive testing in a former chronic drug user without any fMRI imaging and no healthy adult sample; therefore it does not meet the task-based fMRI requirement and lacks a healthy adult group.
This is a qualitative health policy study based on interviews about decision-making at NICE; it is not an fMRI study and does not report task-based brain activation in healthy adults.

Study involves children with Asperger syndrome and ADHD, not healthy adults, and reports neuropsychological/behavioral measures rather than task-based fMRI; does not meet inclusion for adult fMRI problem-solving studies.

This study is an engineering/wastewater methods paper on activated sludge granulation with sensor data; it is not an fMRI or human neuroimaging study and does not include healthy adult participants performing problem-solving tasks. Exclude for non-fMRI/non-human sample.

This study describes a computational spiking-neuron model of inductive reasoning applied to Raven's Progressive Matrices. The abstract gives no indication of task-based fMRI or human participants performing the task, so it is not an fMRI study and contains no healthy adult sample.

Abstract describes a neuropsychological study in children comparing clinical groups (AS, NLD, ADHD) and controls; no mention of task-based fMRI, and participants are children rather than healthy adults. Does not meet fMRI requirement or adult sample criteria.

This is an AI/methods paper about case-based reasoning for wastewater treatment; it does not involve task-based fMRI, reports no human participants or brain activation results. Does not meet inclusion criteria.

Abstract describes a behavioral/psychometric study with 210 participants; no mention of fMRI scanning or task-related brain activation results. This is a non-fMRI study and therefore outside the review scope.

The study is behavioral/psychometric (WISC-IV/WIAT-II linking) using children (WISC = Wechsler Intelligence Scale for Children), not a task-based fMRI study and does not include healthy adults 18–65 or report task-related brain activation. Does not meet fMRI or adult-sample criteria.
Study sampled adolescents (mathematically gifted adolescents) with no adult (18–65) group reported; thus it fails inclusion criterion I3 (no healthy adult sample).

This is a biomaterials/implant study of glaucoma drainage devices with histological and functional implant outcomes, not a task-based fMRI study of problem-solving in healthy adults. No fMRI, no problem-solving task, and no healthy adult sample.

Abstract describes behavioral/computer-administered cognitive tests (TRASI, dual task, Tower of Hanoi) with no mention of fMRI or brain activation results. Study is therefore a non-fMRI study and does not meet inclusion.
The study examines insect/plant biochemistry (whiteflies and salicylic acid), not a human task-based fMRI study. It has no healthy adult participants and is not an fMRI study.

This is an animal (rat) molecular physiology study of Na,K-ATPase overexpression and alveolar fluid clearance; it is not an fMRI study, includes no human participants or healthy adult sample, and reports no task-related brain activation. Exclude for non-fMRI and non-human sample.
This is an educational intervention study measuring exam performance in physiology; no task-based fMRI, brain activation, or neuroimaging methods are mentioned in the abstract.

Abstract reports genetic association analyses of COMT with behavioral traits (intelligence, extraversion) in healthy samples; no fMRI, neuroimaging, or task-based brain activation is described. Excluded as a non-fMRI study.

This is a single clinical case report of a patient with pneumonia and an ectopic kidney; it is not a task-based fMRI study, does not involve problem-solving/reasoning tasks, and the participant is a patient rather than healthy adults. Therefore it fails the review's inclusion criteria and meets exclusion criteria E3 and E4.

Study is a preclinical animal (rat) oncology study of oncolytic virus therapy, not a human task-based fMRI study of problem solving. It does not involve healthy adult human participants nor report fMRI brain activation related to problem solving.

The abstract describes a randomized controlled trial of cognitive and physical activity in older adults and makes no mention of task-based fMRI or task-related brain activation; it is a non-fMRI study and thus excluded.

This is an animal (murine) molecular study of hemophilia-related joint damage; it is not a task-based fMRI study, includes no human participants or problem-solving tasks, and does not report task-related brain activation.
Study is behavioral (cognitive testing), not an fMRI study, and participants are children with sickle cell disease rather than healthy adults. Does not meet fMRI or healthy adult inclusion criteria.

The abstract describes a computational/theoretical neurofuzzy learning method tested on S&P-500 data; it reports no human participants, no task-based fMRI methods, and no task-related brain activation results. Therefore it does not meet inclusion criteria.

The study investigates urinary voiding physiology, not task-based fMRI or problem-solving. It does not report fMRI activation or cognitive problem-solving tasks, so it meets exclusion criterion E4 (non-fMRI study).

This study describes a knowledge-based system for wastewater treatment (engineering application), not a task-based fMRI study; it involves no human participants or fMRI brain activation results. Therefore it does not meet the review criteria.

This is an animal/physiological study of retinal injury in rats (and in vivo animal work), not a human task-based fMRI study of problem solving. It does not involve healthy adult human participants or report fMRI task activation.

The study reports neuropsychological outcomes in pediatric ALL patients comparing steroids; it is not a task-based fMRI study and does not include healthy adult participants performing problem-solving tasks.
This is a psychometric study of the WAIS‑IV test structure, not a task-based fMRI study. No neuroimaging or task-related brain activation is reported, so it fails the fMRI requirement (E4).

This is a behavioral time reproduction study (psychophysics/modeling) with no task-based fMRI, brain activation results, or problem-solving task reported. It does not use fMRI, so it meets exclusion criterion E4.

The study reports cellular experiments in rat hippocampal neurons (animal model) on taurine transport physiology, not task-based fMRI in humans and not a problem-solving task. Therefore it does not include healthy adult human participants and is a non-fMRI study.
This is a materials/drug-release study on ionically cross-linked chitosan films; it does not involve fMRI, brain imaging, or human participants performing problem-solving tasks, so it's not relevant.

Abstract describes behavioral psychometric analyses of cognitive test data aggregated across studies; no mention of task-based fMRI, neuroimaging, or task-related brain activation. Does not meet the fMRI/problem-solving activation criteria.

This is a single-case clinical report of postoperative oliguria/abdominal compartment syndrome, not a task-based fMRI study. It does not include healthy adult participants or report any task-related brain activation.

Clinical case series of rituximab in chronic fatigue syndrome; not a task-based fMRI study, reports no problem-solving task, no healthy adult participants, and no task-related brain activation results.

This study is a psychometric/confirmatory factor analysis of the WAIS-IV in adults with intellectual disabilities and matched controls; it does not involve task-based fMRI or report task-related brain activation, so it does not meet the fMRI requirement for this review.

This is a proteomic/biomarker study of Burkholderia mallei infection, not a task-based fMRI study. It does not involve human healthy adults performing problem-solving tasks or report task-related brain activation. Exclude as non-fMRI and no healthy adult sample.

Abstract describes a single hospitalized trauma patient and nursing education experiences; it is not an fMRI study nor does it involve healthy adult participants performing problem-solving tasks. Therefore it does not meet the review criteria.

This is a preclinical pharmacology/PK-PD study (mice and human biomarker translational work) and does not report any task-based fMRI in healthy adults. It is not an fMRI study, so it fails the review inclusion requirement.

This study reports outcomes of auditory brainstem implantation in children; it is not a task-based fMRI study, does not involve healthy adult participants performing problem-solving/reasoning tasks, and does not report task-related brain activation. Therefore it does not meet the review criteria.

This is a structural diffusion-tensor imaging study in a sample of healthy elderly, not a task-based fMRI study of problem solving in healthy adults aged 18–65. Therefore it does not meet the fMRI/problem-solving inclusion criteria.
Study is an animal (ewe) experimental paper about leptin and bone loss, not a human task-based fMRI study of problem solving. Does not include healthy adult humans or report fMRI task activations.

The abstract reports a psychometric confirmatory factor analysis of intelligence test batteries (KABC-II) and does not involve fMRI or report task-related brain activations. It is not a task-based neuroimaging study.

This is an animal DNA vaccine study in mice, not a task-based fMRI study and does not include healthy adult human participants; it reports immunological outcomes rather than task-related brain activation.

This is a behavioral developmental study (preschoolers and adults) on explanations and predictions about contamination; it does not report any task-based fMRI methods or brain activation results, so it does not meet the fMRI requirement (E4).

Abstract describes behavioral temporal-order perception tasks with cognitive predictors and no mention of fMRI, neuroimaging, or task-related brain activation. Therefore it is not a task-based fMRI study (E4).

This is a historical/philosophical analysis of Boerhaave's nutrition theory, not an empirical task-based fMRI study. The abstract describes no human participants performing problem-solving tasks and reports no fMRI or brain activation results.

The abstract reports a laboratory study of ovarian follicular fluid from IVF patients, not a task-based fMRI experiment. It provides no fMRI or brain-activation data, and the sample are patients rather than a healthy adult group. Exclusion criteria E4 and E3 apply.

This is an analytical chemistry/biochemical methods paper developing a fluorescent gold nanoparticle sensor for glucose in tear fluid. It does not use fMRI, report brain activation, nor involve a problem-solving task, so it fails the fMRI requirement.

This is a biomarker study using CSF from Alzheimer’s patients, not a task-based fMRI investigation of problem solving. It does not involve healthy adult participants performing problem-solving tasks. Exclusion: non-fMRI study and no healthy adult sample.

This study is a structural MRI morphometry investigation in treatment-resistant schizophrenia patients assessing benzodiazepine response; it is not a task-based fMRI study, does not involve problem-solving tasks, and does not include healthy adult participants. Therefore it does not meet the review’s fMRI/problem-solving/healthy adult criteria.

This is a retrospective case series of paediatric cochlear implantation (children mean age 4.7 years) and not a task-based fMRI study of problem solving in healthy adults. It involves a clinical pediatric sample and is not an fMRI study, so it does not meet the review criteria.

This is an engineering/environmental simulation comparing sewer systems; it is not an fMRI study and does not involve human participants performing problem-solving tasks. Excluded for being a non-fMRI/non-human study.

This is an engineering study of primary sludge fermentation and VFA production using a pilot plant; it is not a task-based fMRI study and involves no human participants or brain activation data. Does not meet criteria for fMRI or healthy adult samples.

This is a clinical case report of a single 19-year-old patient with neurological symptoms; it does not describe any task-based fMRI study, includes no healthy adult sample, and does not report task-related brain activation. Thus it clearly fails the fMRI and healthy adult criteria.

This is a theoretical/experimental physics paper on colloidal suspensions with no human participants and no fMRI methods; it does not involve task-based problem-solving in healthy adults. Exclude based on non-fMRI study and no healthy adult sample.

This study is a laboratory pharmacokinetic/antiviral investigation using macrophages and nanoparticles, not a task-based fMRI study of problem solving in healthy adults. It involves in vitro cellular work rather than healthy adult participants and does not report fMRI results.

This is a behavioral/educational study of paramedics’ cannulation intentions; the abstract does not mention fMRI, neuroimaging, or any task-related brain activation results. It therefore fails the task-based fMRI requirement.

This is a physiological study of the pressor response to water drinking in patients (multiple-system atrophy), not a task-based fMRI investigation of problem solving in healthy adults. It is non-fMRI and does not involve a healthy adult problem-solving task.

The abstract describes a simulation/methods paper for wastewater treatment including expert reasoning; it does not report any task-based fMRI data or human participants performing problem-solving tasks. Therefore it is not an fMRI study with healthy adult participants.

This is a roundtable debate/commentary about septic shock management, not a task-based fMRI experiment. It involves clinical patient management (not healthy adult participants) and does not report task-related brain activation. Therefore it meets exclusion criteria for being a review/commentary (E1), having no healthy adult sample (E3), and being a non-fMRI study (E4).

This is a molecular microbiology study in yeast (Saccharomyces cerevisiae), not a task-based fMRI study in healthy adult humans performing problem-solving tasks; therefore it does not meet inclusion criteria and meets exclusion criteria E3 and E4.

This is a surgical case report of a single elderly patient with small bowel obstruction; it is not a task-based fMRI study, does not include healthy adult participants, and reports clinical imaging/surgical findings rather than task-related brain activation.

Abstract describes behavioral study of depressive symptoms and cognitive measures in middle-aged/older adults; no mention of task-based fMRI or task-related brain activation. Therefore it is a non-fMRI study and does not meet inclusion.

This is an engineering study about wastewater treatment and a case-based reasoning tool; it does not involve any fMRI data or human participants performing problem-solving tasks. Excluded for being a non-fMRI study and having no healthy adult sample.

This is a clinical study of diuretic therapy in end-stage renal failure patients under palliative care; it is not a task-based fMRI study and includes no healthy adult participants or brain activation outcomes.

The study is an experimental fluid-mechanics paper using flexible fibers in a soap film; it does not involve task-based fMRI or human participants performing problem-solving. Therefore it is a non-fMRI study and includes no healthy adult sample.
Abstract describes a biochemical characterization of a glycoprotein in fish; not a human task-based fMRI study of problem solving. No human adult participants and not an fMRI study, so excluded.
Abstract describes a theoretical/philosophical discussion of ancient medical theory, not an empirical task-based fMRI study. No human participants or task, and no brain imaging results reported.

This is an engineering/methods paper on case-based reasoning for wastewater treatment (activated sludge). It does not involve fMRI, human participants performing a problem-solving task, or report brain activation. Excluded due to non-fMRI study and no healthy adult sample.

The abstract describes a longitudinal psychometric cohort study (behavioral testing), not a task-based fMRI study and reports no brain activation results. Participants are older adults (ages 55–87), so it does not include the target healthy adult group aged 18–65. Therefore it fails inclusion criteria.

This is an animal (rat) neuroendocrine study investigating galanin/GALP; it is not a human task-based fMRI study and does not include healthy adult participants. Therefore it is non-fMRI and has no human adult sample.

Abstract describes physiological and eye-movement measurements under simulated microgravity; it does not report any fMRI methods, task-based problem-solving tasks, or task-related brain activation. Therefore it is not an fMRI study relevant to the review.

This is a clinical study of bone marrow transplant patients measuring oxidative stress and NF-kappaB, not a task-based fMRI study nor does it include healthy adult participants. Excluded for non-fMRI methodology and absence of healthy adult sample.

The abstract describes a biomechanical model of lymph formation, not a task-based fMRI study of human problem solving. It does not involve human participants performing a reasoning/task nor report brain activation. Therefore it does not meet the review criteria.

This study is an engineering/process monitoring paper on qualitative trend representation for supervisory control in a pilot-scale SBR; it is not a task-based fMRI study, involves no human participants or healthy adult sample, and does not report fMRI brain activation results.

The study is an animal (mouse) experimental study of airway inflammation and not a task-based fMRI study in humans. It does not include healthy adult human participants and is not an fMRI investigation, so it fails the review criteria.
The study is a behavioral task with 8-year-old children (no adult participants) and reports no fMRI data. It therefore does not include healthy adults (E3) and is a non-fMRI study (E4).

The study is an environmental decision-support case study about wastewater treatment for small communities, not a task-based fMRI experiment. It involves no human participants performing problem-solving tasks and reports no fMRI results. Excluded for being a non-fMRI study (E4) and for lacking a healthy adult task-based sample (E3).

This study reports molecular and serological analysis of Borrelia burgdorferi in Lyme disease patients, not an fMRI study of problem-solving. Participants are patients (not healthy adult problem-solvers) and no task-based brain activation or fMRI methods are reported.

This is a clinical case report of inhalation injury; not a task-based fMRI study, does not involve problem-solving tasks, and includes no healthy adult fMRI sample. Therefore it does not meet inclusion criteria.

This is a surgical/clinical study of cavernous body reduction in patients with erectile dysfunction; it is not a task-based fMRI study, does not involve problem-solving tasks, and does not include healthy adult participants.
This is a clinical case report of a single patient with acute eosinophilic pneumonia; it is not an fMRI study, does not involve problem-solving tasks, and does not include healthy adult participants.

This is a microbiology methods study of culture techniques for hemodialysis fluids, not a task-based fMRI study. It includes no human participants performing problem-solving tasks and reports no fMRI or brain activation results.

Abstract describes historical/commentary on kidney transplantation developments, not an empirical task-based fMRI study. No participants performing problem-solving tasks and no healthy adult sample are reported. Therefore it is not eligible for the review.

This is a clinical case report of acute empyema in a pregnant patient. It is not an fMRI study, does not involve any task-based problem solving or reasoning tasks, and does not include healthy adult participants. Exclusion is clear from the abstract.
